body.title: [triage] Ticket filenames lead with status for ls-by-status (post-cutover)
body.description:
**⛔ FAIL — KICKED BACK TO DESIGN 2026-07-13 (Akien: 'those should get sent back to the design step as FAIL for learning from and fixing').**

**THIS TICKET ENTERED SPRINT WITH NO INTENTION.** Under D-intentions-are-the-root-2026-07-13 and proof-on-close, THE INTENTION IS THE PROPERTY MUTATION-RED BREAKS. With no intention there is **no property to break**, so this ticket **cannot be proven as it stands** — it could only ever close hollow, or `shipped-unproven` for a reason that is not the real one.

**IT IS NOT DEFECTIVE WORK — IT IS INCOMPLETE DESIGN.** So the honest disposition is BACK TO DESIGN, not a backfilled intention. CC drafting the missing intention would have **papered over the real defect**: 150 tickets cleared the design step without an intention, which is a **gate hole**, not 150 individual oversights. Per CLAUDE.md, the nuisance stays VISIBLE so it drives finding the fix — never papered over, which sucks silently forever (CP4).

**CP2 — FAIL = FURTHER ADVANCE IN LEARNING.** This kickback IS the learning signal. The fix is upstream: the triage→sprint transition must reject a missing, empty, or degenerate (`TBD`/`unknown`) intention (T-sprint-tickets-with-no-intention-cannot-be-proven).

**TO RE-ENTER SPRINT:** state the intention as a present-tense contract — *'I intend that X, because Y'* — where X names a property a hollow build would violate.

**AND NOTE WHO FAILED HERE.** Akien wrote none of these tickets. **CC wrote all 149.** So 'the design step that failed' is CC, and the honest rule is not 'a human must declare it' — it is: **THE AUTHOR DECLARES THE INTENTION AT AUTHORING TIME, FROM THE CONVERSATION THAT PRODUCED THE TICKET.** That is what makes it `declared`: not that a human typed it (a ratified string and a machine-written string are byte-identical), but that it was stated from evidence INDEPENDENT of the artifact — the design conversation, which traces to Akien's intent. An intention RECONSTRUCTED LATER FROM THE TICKET BODY has no independent evidence and is `extracted-from-artifact`: a description, never a spec (D-provenance-is-the-safety-property-2026-07-13). **The failure was not forgetting a field. It was filing 149 tickets without ever pausing to say what I intended by them.**

---

**Akien's ask (2026-06-19):** ticket filenames should lead with status (status--title--createdatetime) so the active queue is filterable by a plain `ls`/directory search, and the original creation date is visible without extended file properties.

**Current:** `cc.0.T-<slug>.<YYYYMMDD.HHMMSSffffff>.json` — already carries the created datestamp AND the title-slug; only the leading status is missing.

**Proposed shape:** `<status>.cc.0.T-<slug>.<created-stamp>.json` (status FIRST). Keep the `T-<slug>` so `_find` keeps globbing by id fast (dropping it for free text forces a scan+parse of every file on the 2000+ store).

**Consequences to handle:**
- Status-in-name = rename on EVERY status transition (today only the terminal ->closed/ move renames). ticket_store already does atomic os.replace for the closed move; extend that single mover (_put) to rename within tickets/ when the status component changes. Atomic-rename + flock invariants must hold.
- Does NOT replace the closed/ bin: the subdir keeps the active dir holding only in-flight work (hard requirement); the status prefix makes `ls` within each dir filterable. Keep both.
- One-time migration: rename ~2088 existing files to the new scheme.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/ticket_store.py (_envelope filename, _put rename-on-status-change, _find/_iter_files glob), a one-shot migration script.
**Design rules:** filesystem-first build queue (D-build-queue-filesystem-first); atomic write+rename (PATTERN-004); closed/ bin keeps active dir clean.
**Scope boundary:** filename scheme + rename-on-transition + one-time migration; does NOT change the envelope body schema or the closed/ bin decision.
**Test plan:** ls-by-status returns the right set; rename-on-transition leaves a single copy with the new name; _find still resolves by id; migration is idempotent.

**Sequencing (CC recommendation, Akien approved 'when you think best'):** GATED on T-ticket-pg-drop — do the filename rename AFTER the authority flip lands, so we are not migrating 2088 filenames while also flipping the source of truth. One big change at a time, per the decision's no-big-bang rule.
**HIGH-inertia:** ticket_store.py chokepoint + bulk file migration. Approval pre-stamped (Akien, 2026-06-19, this design conversation).
body.fail_reason: entered sprint with no intention — cannot be proven; design incomplete
